Phronesis is an independent research entity producing thorough and accessible work at the intersection of technology law and policy, for the casual reader and experts alike.

Appetite for rigor
What we publish

Research Papers

Peer-quality scholarship on technology governance and digital law.

Policy Proposals

Concrete, actionable frameworks for regulators and lawmakers.

Legal Analysis

Close readings of cases, statutes, and regulatory actions shaping tech.

Essays

Argumentative, idea-driven writing that provokes and persuades.

Articles

Timely, concise takes on current events in tech policy and litigation.

Reports

Comprehensive reference documents synthesizing research for key audiences.
Featured Work All publications
Research Paper

Governing the Stack: Regulatory Jurisdiction in Multi-Layer AI Systems

Maps jurisdictional gaps across AI model layers, fine-tuning, APIs, and deployment contexts, proposing a tiered liability framework.

March 2025 Read
Legal Analysis

Section 230 After NetChoice: What the Court Left Unsaid

A close reading of the 2024 NetChoice opinions, the doctrinal questions they resolved, and the larger questions they deliberately avoided.

Jan 2025 Read

On Phronesis

Wisdom, not just knowledge.

The name came from a book that I read not too long ago, but unfortunately the title has already been forgotten, however the word has stayed. Phronesis: Aristotle's term for practical wisdom — familiar to some, entirely foreign to others. Which feels like the right kind of name for what we are building.

This is a home for a particular perspective, produced by people with backgrounds spanning academia, industry, and government. We publish what we find worth saying, for readers who find it worth reading. Nothing more than that.
